div1.entryFree:
Wasteful, 1) lavish, profuse: to add another hue unto the rainbow . . . is w. and ridiculous excess, John IV, 2, 16. hath seized the w. king, R2 III, 4, 55. I have retired me to a w. cock, Tim. II, 2, 171.
2) ruinous, destructive, consumptive: where w. time debateth with decay, Sonn. 15, 11. when w. war shall statues overturn, 55, 5. lean and w. learning, As III, 2, 341. w. vengeance, H5 I, 2, 283. the wild and w. ocean, III, 1, 14. for ruin's w. entrance, Mcb. II, 3, 120.
